data.title: Microsoft Stock Falls Despite Q2 Earnings Beat — What Spooked Investors
data.description:
Microsoft (MSFT) fell in after-hours trading after its latest earnings report raised fresh doubts about near-term cloud and AI growth, even as the company beat Wall Street estimates.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The tech giant reported adjusted earnings of $4.14 per share, above estimates of $3.91. Revenue rose 17% from a year ago to $81.27 billion, also topping forecasts. Still, the stock lost over 6% in after-hours trading on Wednesday as the market looked beyond the headline numbers.

Cloud Growth Was Solid, but Concerns Remain

Commercial cloud revenue reached $51.5 billion, slightly ahead of estimates. The Intelligent Cloud segment, which includes Azure, posted $32.9 billion in revenue, also beating forecasts.

However, investors appeared cautious about how fast that growth can continue. Microsoft said demand for AI services is rising faster than its current capacity, which is limiting near-term revenue gains.

AI Spending Pressures the Near Term

Microsoft made it clear that heavy spending will continue as it works to close that gap. The company said capital spending will rise further in fiscal 2026 after investing $88.2 billion last year, largely to support AI demand.

Management said the spending is needed to support AI workloads and data center buildouts. While that strengthens Microsoft's long-term position, it also raised concerns about near-term margins and returns, which weighed on the stock.

What Investors Will Watch Next

The focus now shifts to execution. Investors want to see when new data-center capacity comes online and whether AI demand can turn into faster cloud revenue growth.

Until supply catches up and spending levels stabilize, strong earnings alone may not be enough to lift the stock.

What Is the Price Target for Microsoft? 

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Strong Buy consensus rating on MSFT stock based on 32 Buys and two Sells assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. Furthermore, the average MSFT price target of $618.64 per share implies 28.45% upside potential.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: Tesla Stock (TSLA) Jumps after Musk Scraps Premium Model S/X, Invests $2B in xAI
data.description:
Tesla (TSLA) shares jumped 2.2% in after-hours trading yesterday after CEO Elon Musk's Q4FY25 earnings call announcements. Musk said that Tesla is going to stop producing higher-end versions of Model S and X to free up space for producing Optimus robots, as the company moves ahead to an "autonomous future."

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Tesla beat Q4 expectations despite a 3% year-over-year sales drop to $24.90 billion, with full-year 2025 revenue falling 3% to $94.8 billion. Earnings per share were $0.50, down from the prior year's figure of $0.73, but it beat the consensus of $0.45.

Tesla Invests in xAI

Musk stated that Tesla will invest $2 billion in his privately held artificial intelligence (AI) startup xAI, despite mixed shareholder views. Tesla entered into an agreement on January 16 to invest in xAI's Series E funding round. Shareholders had last year voted down a proposal urging the board to invest in the startup, with more "no" votes than "yes." Musk's other venture, SpaceX, also invested $2 billion in xAI, a competitor to OpenAI.

Why Halt Model S and X?

Sales of Model S sedan and Model X SUV have declined over the past year, despite interior and exterior refreshes. Tesla always intended its more affordable models, the Model 3 sedan and Model Y SUV, to vastly outsell their predecessors. The company faces stiffer competition in the luxury EV market from legacy automakers, as well as upstarts like Rivian (RIVN) and Lucid Motors (LCID).

Both models are built at Tesla's Fremont, California factory. Once production ends there, Tesla will use the same space to build Optimus robots, with a long-term target of producing one million robots annually. Meanwhile, Cybertruck production will continue at the company's factory outside Austin.

Musk said the company will produce the final versions of both EVs next quarter, and it will continue supporting existing Model S and Model X owners for "as long as people have the vehicles."

Is Tesla a Buy, Sell, or Hold?

On TipRanks, Tesla has a Hold consensus rating based on 10 Buys, eight Holds, and six Sell ratings. The average Tesla price target of $407.35 implies 5.6% downside potential from current levels. Over the past year, TSLA shares have gained nearly 11%.
data.author: Sheryl Sheth

data.title: Meta's Q4 Earnings Calm AI Spend Fears — Why the Stock Is Moving Higher. Top Analysts Weigh In
data.description:
Meta (META) stock gained over 6% in after-hours trading on Wednesday after posting fourth-quarter results that topped Wall Street expectations and helped calm worries around AI spending. The report showed strong ad demand and guidance that came in better than feared, even as the company plans to keep investing heavily.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

For context, Meta reported earnings of $8.88 per share, beating the Street's $8.23 estimate. Revenue for the quarter came in at $59.89 billion, also above expectations of $58.59 billion. Meanwhile, daily active people across Meta's apps reached 3.58 billion, in line with forecasts.

Ads and AI Are Working Together

While the earnings beat was solid, the bigger takeaway was how AI is lifting Meta's ad business. Ad impressions delivered across Meta's Family of Apps rose about 18% year-over-year, and the average price per ad increased about 6%, showing continued strength in ad demand on Facebook and Instagram.

Meta has been using AI to improve ad targeting and content feeds, which has helped drive engagement and ad volume. That progress is helping offset concerns that rising AI costs would weigh too much on profits.

Guidance Helped Shift the Tone

Meta's outlook also played a key role in the market reaction. For the first quarter, the company guided revenue to a range of $53.5 billion to $56.5 billion, well ahead of Wall Street's $51.41 billion estimate.

At the same time, Meta said it expects 2026 total expenses to fall between $162 billion and $169 billion, with much of the spending tied to AI and infrastructure. While those numbers are high, they came in slightly below what some investors had feared.

What Top Analysts Are Saying

Wall Street reactions were mostly positive. Top Goldman Sachs analyst Eric Sheridan reiterated a Buy rating with an $815 price target, noting solid revenue trends and cost guidance that came in below fears. He added that the results give Meta a chance to explain how its AI plans and core business can support growth over the next year.

Likewise, Stifel analyst Mark Kelley also kept a Buy rating and a $785 price target, pointing to beats on revenue, margins, and earnings. The five-star analyst highlighted strong ad impression growth and first-quarter guidance that came in well above expectations.

Is META Stock a Buy? 

The stock of Meta Platforms has a consensus Strong Buy rating among 42 Wall Street analysts. That rating is based on 37 Buy and five Hold recommendations issued in the last three months. The average META price target of $828.43 implies 23.88% upside from current levels. 

Read more analyst ratings on META Stock
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: Why Is Joby Aviation Stock (JOBY) Down Over 10% Today?
data.description:
Joby Aviation (JOBY), the electric air taxi company, fell more than 10% in after-hours trading on Wednesday after announcing plans to raise fresh capital.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The company said it aims to raise about $1 billion through a sale of common stock and convertible senior notes due 2032. The secondary stock offering is expected to be priced between $11.35 and $11.75 per share. Joby said the funds will be used to support aircraft certification, production work, and long-term growth plans.

The announcement pushed the stock lower as investors reacted to dilution risk tied to the new share issuance.

Why Investors Reacted Negatively

Stock offerings often pressure shares because they increase the total share count, which can lower the value of existing holdings. For early-stage companies like Joby, which are still investing heavily ahead of commercial launches, dilution worries tend to surface quickly.

While the added cash strengthens Joby's balance sheet and supports its long-term goals, the size and timing of the raise triggered a sharp near-term selloff.

What Investors Should Look For Next

Joby has been an active name in the eVTOL space. The stock has gained 60.5% over the past year, helped by progress toward certification and growing interest in air taxi services.

The key focus now is how Joby uses the new capital. Investors will watch for updates on FAA certification, production timelines, and testing progress, along with any news on partnerships and planned service launches.

Is Joby Aviation Stock a Good Buy?

The Street analysts are relatively optimistic about the company's prospects. Based on six recent ratings, Joby Aviation boasts a "Hold" consensus with an average JOBY stock price target of $15.67. This implies a 17.20% upside from the current price.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: Stock Market Today: SPX Retraces Below Record-High $7,000 as Fed Holds Rates Steady
data.description:
The S&P 500 (SPX) closed slightly in the red on Wednesday after the Fed voted 8-2 to maintain interest rates between 3.50% and 3.75%. The index briefly surpassed an all-time high of 7,000 in early-morning trading, although it ended the day below the psychological price level.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Fed Governors Stephen Miran and Christopher Waller were the two dissenting officials, voting in favor of a 25 bps rate cut. Waller's vote was closely scrutinized given his candidacy for the Fed Chair position. A vote in favor of lower rates could support his odds of being nominated by President Trump, who has repeatedly advocated for aggressive rate cuts. Waller's odds of being nominated by Trump jumped to 12% from 9.5% after the Fed announced its decision.

During the following press conference, Fed Chair Jerome Powell highlighted resilient consumer spending alongside a surprisingly strong economy. "The outlook for economic activity has clearly improved since the last meeting," he said. In another bright spot, Powell expects tariff-driven inflation to peak sometime during the middle of the year before coming down. A strong economy with falling inflation lowers the case for additional rate cuts. Following these comments, the odds of a 25 bps reduction at the March 18 Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) meeting fell to 11.9% from 16.7% a day ago on CME's FedWatch tool.

At the same time, Powell emphasized that low job openings and hiring rates point to continued risks in the labor market. In addition, he believes that AI could displace some jobs in the near term, with the unemployment rate for recent college graduates already facing an impact.

Meanwhile, tensions between the U.S. and Iran are on the rise after Trump emphasized that a "massive Armada is heading to Iran," and urged the country to sign a deal to scrap its nuclear development program. "Time is running out, it is truly of the essence!" Trump said in a Truth Social post. "As I told Iran once before, MAKE A DEAL!"

Elsewhere, gold has handily outperformed the S&P 500 over the past year, returning 90% compared to the benchmark index at 15%. That doesn't bode well for stocks, according to Stifel Chief Equity Strategist Barry Bannister. Such periods of significant outperformance from the precious metal have happened only four times during the past century, and each was followed by poor stock returns. "The S&P 500 was range-bound for years after the S&P 500 relative to gold crossed this threshold in the past," wrote Bannister.

The S&P 500 (SPX) closed with a less than 0.01% loss, while the Nasdaq 100 (NDX) returned 0.32%.

Stay ahead of macro events with our up-to-the-minute Economic Calendar — filter by impact, country, and more.
data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: Palantir Technologies (PLTR) Q4 Earnings on Deck: Options Market Braces for a 10.70% Swing
data.description:

data.title: Intel Stock Jumps 11% and CFO May Think It's Time to Buy
data.description:
Intel (NASDAQ:INTC) shares surged 11% on Wednesday as an upbeat outlook from Texas Instruments reignited a broader semiconductor rally, easing fears that the recent selloff in chip stocks was a warning of weakening demand in an AI-driven market.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The move comes just days after Intel's fourth-quarter results sent the stock sharply lower. While the company beat expectations for the quarter, its forward guidance failed to impress. Intel forecast first-quarter revenue of $11.7 billion to $12.7 billion and adjusted EPS of $0.00, both below Wall Street's expectations for revenue of $12.56 billion and adjusted EPS of $0.04.

Against the backdrop of an ongoing AI boom, that was not the message investors were hoping to hear, and shares slid 17% in the following trading session.

However, evidently one notable insider decided the time was right to load up at a discount. This week, CFO David Zinsner bought 5,882 shares for $247,392. As the saying goes, insiders will often sell stock for various reasons but will only buy due to one: they think the shares are bound to push higher.

That view aligns with Seaport analyst Jay Goldberg, who argues that even with the softer near-term outlook, Intel is still heading in the right strategic direction.

"Obviously the guidance was not great, but we actually came away from the call encouraged by many of the things the company said," Goldberg opined. "Compare this call to six months ago, and management's outlook is night and day."

Essentially, the core issue is that demand is running ahead of what the company can currently supply. "A problem," concedes Goldberg, "but a good problem to have."

Much of the Q&A focused on these constraints, and like the rest of the industry, the company is dealing with tight memory availability, but management also flagged shortages in substrates and other inputs. Importantly, notes Goldberg, these issues are manageable. Unlike many peers in the semi industry, Intel's constraints are not tied to TSMC, and management expects supply limitations to ease sequentially through the rest of the year.

"It has been a very long time since Intel has 'suffered' from too much demand," Goldberg added.

Bottom line, Goldberg is firmly in Intel's corner, giving the stock a Buy rating and a $65 price target. If he's right, that leaves room for about 17% upside from today's levels. (To watch Goldberg's track record, click here)

The wider Street is more cautious. 7 other analysts are bullish, but that's offset by 20 Holds and 4 Sells, leaving Intel with a consensus Hold (i.e., Neutral) rating. Based on the $48.36 average price target, shares are expected to be trading at roughly the same level a year from now. (See INTC stock forecast)
data.author: Marty Shtrubel

data.title: U.S. Stock Futures Slip as Gold Hits New Highs amid Big Tech Earnings, MSFT Slides and META Stock Gains
data.description:
U.S. stock futures fell Wednesday night as investors reacted to rising gold prices. Traders also weighed earnings reports from major tech firms, namely Meta (META), Microsoft (MSFT), and Tesla (TSLA), along with the Federal Reserve's latest rate decision. Futures on the Nasdaq 100 (NDX), the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA), and the S&P 500 (SPX) Index were down 0.10%, 0.03%, and 0.18%, respectively, at 8:30 p.m. EDT on January 28.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Spot gold jumped past $5,500 in overnight trading, hitting new highs as the U.S. dollar continues to weaken.

In after-hours trading, Meta rose 7% after the Facebook parent issued a stronger-than-expected sales outlook for the first quarter. In contrast, Microsoft shares slid 6% after slower cloud growth in the fiscal second quarter and softer margin guidance for the current quarter. Tesla edged up 1% after its fourth-quarter results topped expectations.

In Wednesday's regular session, the S&P 500 briefly crossed the 7,000 mark but finished little changed after the Fed left interest rates unchanged at 3.5% to 3.75%. The Dow added just 12 points, while the Nasdaq Composite gained about 0.2%, helped by gains in Nvidia (NVDA) and Micron Technology (MU).

Looking ahead, investors are waiting for Apple's (AAPL) fiscal first-quarter earnings on Thursday after the close. Mastercard (MA), Caterpillar (CAT), and Lockheed Martin (LMT) are also due to report.

On the data front, markets will focus on weekly jobless claims, durable goods orders, and wholesale inventories.

data.title: Tesla Stock (NASDAQ:TSLA) Notches Up With Pilot Partnership
data.description:
While electric vehicle giant Tesla's (TSLA) status as an electric vehicle giant is under fire these days, there are some signs its move to be part of the electric semi race is taking shape. Tesla recently launched a partnership effort with Pilot Travel Centers to bring charging stations to the truck stops. The move proved welcome for investors, who sent shares up fractionally in Wednesday afternoon's trading.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Tesla will bring its line of Semi Chargers to several Pilot stations, some of which will be active as early as this summer. Most of the stops will be along I-5, I-10, and "several major corridors where the need for heavy-duty charging is highest." The Pilot stations that have Tesla Semi Chargers will have them in blocks of four to eight, reports note, and use the V4 cabinet charging technology to deliver power faster.

Pilot's senior vice president of alternative fuels, Shannon Sturgil, noted, "Helping to shape the future of energy is a strategic pillar in meeting the needs of our guests and the North American transportation industry. Heavy-duty charging is yet another extension of our exploration into alternative fuel offerings, and we're happy to partner with a leader in the space that provides turnkey solutions and deploys them quickly."

Puzzling Name Change May Be Ahead

Tesla is also planning a move that might puzzle some car buyers, as it sets out to change the name of the Model Y, but only the base model of the Model Y. Thankfully, the name change here should prove helpful to most, as Tesla renames the Model Y Standard to the Model Y Rear-Wheel Drive. Oddly, the other model in the Model Y series, the Model Y Premium, also comes with rear-wheel drive, so the distinction might be a bit puzzling.

Tesla, meanwhile, has not confirmed the name change, so this may never actually happen. Those interested in buying a Tesla will need to check the website, or hit a dealership, to find out if the name change has gone through. Still, why Tesla suddenly feels the need to highlight a feature on the Standard so heavily is, at best, unclear.

Is Tesla a Buy, Hold or Sell?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Hold consensus rating on TSLA stock based on 10 Buys, eight Holds, and six Sells assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. After a 10.74% rally in its share price over the past year, the average TSLA price target of $406.87 per share implies 6.05% downside risk.
data.author: Steve Anderson
